name: 05 — Job Discovery Run (Scheduled + Manual)
_comment: ARCHITECTURE NOTE: n8n does NOT re-implement scoring, dedup, classification, or discovery profile logic. It only triggers POST /discover on the Netlify function. Set SITE_URL and DISCOVERY_SECRET env vars in n8n. DISCOVERY_SECRET must match the server env var of the same name. TIMEOUT FIX (2026-04): Each source family now has its own HTTP Request node so a slow or failed source is isolated and cannot crash the whole workflow. Guard Code nodes validate the response shape before reading fields, preventing 'Unknown error' when a 504 HTML page is received instead of JSON.

_daily_schedule.note: Changed from every-6h to daily. Each source family (Lever, Greenhouse) runs as a separate HTTP call so a single-source timeout does not abort the whole run.

_env_vars_required.SITE_URL: Your Netlify site URL, e.g. https://your-app.netlify.app
_env_vars_required.DISCOVERY_SECRET: Must match DISCOVERY_SECRET env var set in Netlify — required for /discover auth

_notes: This workflow fires daily at 7am UTC. Adjust the schedule in 'Schedule Trigger (Daily 7am UTC)' as needed.
Manual trigger is available for on-demand discovery runs from the n8n UI.
DISCOVERY_SECRET is passed as X-Discovery-Secret header — must match server env var.
All scoring, dedup, and classification happen inside the Netlify function. n8n only triggers it.

TIMEOUT FIX (2026-04): The workflow now sends two separate POST /discover calls — one for Lever (sourceFamily:lever) and one for Greenhouse (sourceFamily:greenhouse). Each call is isolated: a 504 or timeout on one source does not crash the other. Guard Code nodes catch non-JSON responses (e.g. 504 HTML pages) and produce a clean error object. The Merge node combines both results and logs source-level errors.
If /discover returns 401, check DISCOVERY_SECRET in both n8n and Netlify environments.
If discovery is disabled (LIVE_INTAKE_ENABLED != true), each source-family call returns ok:false with a message — n8n logs and continues.
LEVER SUPPORT: Set LEVER_BOARDS env var (comma-separated company slugs, e.g. atlassian,canva) in Netlify. Enable the src-lever-boards source in the Sources UI.
GREENHOUSE SUPPORT: Set GREENHOUSE_BOARDS env var (comma-separated board tokens) in Netlify. Enable the src-greenhouse-boards source in the Sources UI.
SOURCE-SPECIFIC TESTING: Each source family now has its own HTTP Request node. To test a single family, run the corresponding 'POST /discover — <Family>' node manually from the n8n UI.
GREENHOUSE-ONLY RUN: Use the 'POST /discover — Greenhouse' node (body: {"sourceFamily":"greenhouse"}) for an isolated Greenhouse test.
LEVER-ONLY RUN: Use the 'POST /discover — Lever' node (body: {"sourceFamily":"lever"}) for an isolated Lever test.
USAJOBS-ONLY RUN: Add a 'POST /discover — USAJobs' node with body {"sourceFamily":"usajobs"} — only works when USAJOBS_API_KEY and USAJOBS_USER_AGENT env vars are set and src-usajobs is enabled.

LEVER ACTIVATION ORDER: (1) Set LEVER_BOARDS in Netlify env, (2) enable src-lever-boards in Sources UI, (3) run this workflow manually once, (4) verify queue quality, (5) run a second time to confirm dedup, (6) only then keep the schedule active.
DO NOT activate multiple new source families simultaneously. Prove Lever clean before enabling others.

ROLLBACK: To disable Lever only, disable src-lever-boards in the Sources UI or set LEVER_BOARDS= (empty) in Netlify and redeploy. Greenhouse remains unaffected. The Lever guard node will return ok:false with a clear message.

ADDING MORE SOURCES (USAJobs, RSS): Add a new 'POST /discover — <Family>' HTTP Request node + Guard Code node pair after the Greenhouse guard, then update the Merge node to include the new result. Do not change the Merge node's reference to $('Guard Lever Response') or $('Guard Greenhouse Response').

GUARD NODE SYNC: The 'Guard Lever Response' and 'Guard Greenhouse Response' Code nodes contain identical logic (try/catch JSON parse, 401 check, ok:false check, demo check) — only the source_family label differs. n8n Code nodes cannot share functions across nodes. If you update the guard logic in one node, update the other identically.

USAJOBS ACTIVATION (Wave 2): (1) Register key at developer.usajobs.gov, (2) set USAJOBS_API_KEY and USAJOBS_USER_AGENT in Netlify, (3) enable src-usajobs in Sources UI, (4) add a POST /discover — USAJobs node with body {"sourceFamily":"usajobs"}, (5) verify quality and dedup before including in daily schedule.
SOURCE ACTIVATION WAVES: Wave 1 (active) = Lever + Greenhouse. Wave 2 (staged) = USAJobs. Wave 3 (staged) = RSS. Do not activate Wave 2/3 until Wave 1 is clean and stable.

QUALITY GATE: Before enabling any new source family in the daily schedule, it must pass: (1) manual run with real roles, (2) second-run dedup (zero re-ingest), (3) recommended_pct >= 30% with >= 5 records in Reports > Source Quality.
